IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PROVIDING AFFORDABLE RENTAL HOUSING THAT EMPOWERS LOW-INCOME RESIDENTS AND CHANGES COMMUNITIES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: TO PROVIDE AWARD-WINNING AFFORDABLE HOUSING PAIRED WITH BEST-PRACTICE SOCIAL SERVICE PROGRAMS TAILORED TO HELP FAMILIES MOVE TOWARD SELF-SUFFICIENCY AND ULTIMATELY TO HOME OWNERSHIP.
IRS990.Desc: IMPROVING THE QUALITY OF LIFE FOR LOW-INCOME FAMILIES BY STABILIZING AND REVITALIZING NEIGHBORHOODS VIA ACQUISITION, RENOVATION, AND MANAGEMENT OF AFFORDABLE HOUSING DEVELOPMENTS.

OtherLiabilitiesOrgGrp.Desc: K-1 INVESTMENTS, NET NEGATIVE BASIS BALANCE

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION IS EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL INCOME TAXES UNDER INTERNAL REVENUE CODE SECTION 501(C)(3) AND FROM CALIFORNIA INCOME TAXES UNDER RTC SECTION 23701(D). THE PREPARATION OF FINANCIAL STATEMENTS IN ACCORDANCE WITH ACCOUNTING PRINCIPLES GENERALLY ACCEPTED IN THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA REQUIRES THE ORGANIZATION TO REPORT INFORMATION REGARDING ITS EXPOSURE TO VARIOUS TAX POSITIONS TAKEN BY THE ORGANIZATION. MANAGEMENT HAS DETERMINED WHETHER ANY TAX POSITIONS HAVE MET THE RECOGNITION THRESHOLD AND HAS MEASURED THE ORGANIZATION'S EXPOSURE TO THOSE TAX POSITIONS. MANAGEMENT BELIEVES THAT THE ORGANIZATION HAS ADEQUATELY ADDRESSED ALL RELEVANT TAX POSITIONS AND THAT THERE ARE NO UNRECORDED TAX LIABILITIES. FEDERAL AND STATE TAX AUTHORITIES GENERALLY HAVE THE RIGHT TO EXAMINE AND AUDIT THE PREVIOUS THREE YEARS OF TAX RETURNS FILED. ANY INTEREST OR PENALTIES ASSESSED TO THE ORGANIZATION ARE RECORDED IN OPERATING EXPENSES. NO INTEREST OR PENALTIES FROM FEDERAL OR STATE TAX AUTHORITIES WERE RECORDED IN THE ACCOMPANYING FINANCIAL STATEMENTS.
THE BOXES CHECKED IN 1(A) OF PART I WERE PROVIDED BY RELATED ORGANIZATIONS. ALL BOARD MEMBERS ARE COMPENSATED BY A RELATED ORGANIZATION FOR COMMERCIAL AIRLINE TRAVEL AND TRAVEL FOR COMPANIONS.
A RELATED ORGANIZATION USES A WRITTEN EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT IN ESTABLISHING THE COMPENSATION OF THE EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 2
FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE 990 IS REVIEWED BY THE CONTROLLER AND THEN GIVEN TO THE CHIEF FINANICAL OFFICER FOR REVIEW AND SIGNATURE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EACH DIRECTOR IS PROVIDED A COPY OF THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY FOR THE CORPORATION AND SIGNS AN ACKNOWLEDGEMENT CERTIFICATE. STAFF IS ALSO PROVIDED A COPY AND SIGNS AN ACKNOWLEDGEMENT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS REVIEW AND A THIRD PARTY COMPENSATION STUDY IS PERFORMED EVERY 2 TO 3 YEARS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE ORGANIZATION MAKES ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC UPON REQUEST.

THE OFFICERS SPEND APPROXIMATELY 20-30 HOURS PER WEEK DEVOTED TO THIS ORGANIZATION AND APPROXIMATELY 10-20 HOURS PER WEEK DEVOTED TO RELATED ORGANIZATIONS. THEIR TIME VARIES DEPENDING ON THE NEEDS OF THE EXEMPT ORGANIZATION.
THE CORPORATE SECRETARY IS RESPONSIBLE FOR DEVELOPING AND MAINTAINING THE MINUTES OF ALL MEETINGS OF THE GOVERNING BODY AND COMMITTEES WITH AUTHORITY ON ITS BEHALF.
THE ORGANIZATION'S OVERSIGHT/SELECTION PROCESS DIDN'T CHANGE FROM THE PRIOR YEAR.
